sections.title: 10.6Resource MolecularSequence - Content
sections.introduction:
Raw data describing a biological sequence.
This resource is referenced by itself and Observation
sections.Scope and Usage:
The MolecularSequence resource is designed to describe an atomic sequence which contains the alignment sequencing test result and multiple variations. Atomic sequences can be connected by link element and they will lead to sequence graph. By this method, a sequence can be reported. Complete genetic sequence information, of which specific genetic variations are a part, is reported by reference to the GA4GH repository.  Thus, the FHIR MolecularSequence resource avoids large genomic payloads in a manner analogous to how the FHIR ImagingStudy resource references large images maintained in other systems.
    For use cases, details on how this resource interact with other Clinical Genomics resources or profiles, please refer to implementation guidance document here .
Variant Databases: dbSNP , ClinVar , and COSMIC Reference Sequences: RefSeq and ENSEMBL
This resource is designed to describe sequence variations with clinical significance with information such as:
Name of the variation represented Type of the variation Gene region occupied by the variation Tissue source used to determine genotype of the variation Quality of the result
It is strongly encouraged to provide all available information in this resource for any reported variants, because receiving systems (e.g. discovery research, outcomes analysis, and public health reporting) may use this information to normalize variants over time or across sources.  However, these data should not be used to dynamically correct/change variant representations for clinical use outside of the laboratory, due to insufficient information.
Implementers should be aware that semantic equivalency of results of genetic variants cannot be guaranteed unless there is an agreed upon standard between sending and receiving systems.
sections.Boundaries and Relationships:
Focus of the resource is to provide sequencing alignment data immediately relevant to what the interpretation on clinical decision-making originates from. Hence data such as precise read of DNA sequences and sequence alignment are not included; such data are nonetheless accessible through references to GA4GH (Global Alliance for Genomics and Health) API.
    
    The MolecularSequence resource will be referenced by Observation to provide variant information. As clinical assessments/diagnosis of a patient are typically captured in the Condition resource or the ClinicalImpression resource, the MolecularSequence resource can be referenced by the Condition resource to provide specific genetic data to support assertions. This is analogous to how Condition references other resources, such as AllergyIntolerance , Procedure , and Questionnaire resources.
sections.Notes:
When saving the variant information, the nucleic acid will be numbered with order. Some files are using 0-based coordinates (e.g. BCD file format) while some files are using 1-based coordinates (e.g. VCF file format). The element coordinateSystem in MolecularSequence resource contains this information.
MolecularSequence.coordinateSystem constraints within two possible values: 0 for 0-based system, which will mark the sequence from number 0, while 1 for 1-based system, which will begin marking the first position with number 1. The significant difference between two system is the end position. In 0-based system, the end position is exclusive , which means the last position will not be contained in the sequence window while in 1-based system, the end position is inclusive , which means the last position is included in the sequence window. Note both systems has an inclusive start position.
For example, ACGTGCAT will be numbered from 1 to 8 in 1-based system and will be numbered from 0 to 8 in 0-based system to mark flanks (i.e. place between two Nucleotide). So the interval [3,5] in 1-based system is GTG while interval [2,5) in 0-based system is same segment GTG.
There are lots of definition concerning with the Directionality of DNA or RNA. Here we are using referenceSeq.orientation and referenceSeq.strand . orientation represents the sense of the sequence, which has different meanings depending on the MolecularSequence.type . strand represents the sequence writing order. Watson strand refers to 5' to 3' top strand (5' -> 3'), whereas Crick strand refers to 5' to 3' bottom strand (3' <- 5').
We hope that string of observedSeq can be constrained more than just any normal string but with notation tables. Here we present what the nucleotide acid string should be constrained within the range: A --> adenosine M --> A C (amino) U --> uridine H --> A C T V --> G C A C --> cytidine S --> G C (strong) D --> G A T K --> G T (keto) G --> guanine W --> A T (weak) R --> G A (purine) N --> A G C T (any) T --> thymidine B --> G T C Y --> T C (pyrimidine) - --> gap of indeterminate length while the amino acid string should be constrained within the range: A alanine P proline B aspartate or asparagine Q glutamine C cystine R arginine D aspartate S serine E glutamate T threonine F phenylalanine U selenocysteine G glycine V valine H histidine W tryptophan I isoleucine Y tyrosine K lysine Z glutamate or glutamine L leucine X any M methionine * translation stop N asparagine - gap of indeterminate length

definitions:
MolecularSequence: raw data describing a biological sequence. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.identifier: a unique identifier for this particular sequence instance. this is a fhir-defined id. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.type: amino acid sequence/ dna sequence / rna sequence. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.coordinateSystem: whether the sequence is numbered starting at 0 (0-based numbering or coordinates, inclusive start, exclusive end) or starting at 1 (1-based numbering, inclusive start and inclusive end). [STOP]
MolecularSequence.patient: the patient whose sequencing results are described by this resource. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.specimen: specimen used for sequencing. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.device: the method for sequencing, for example, chip information. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.performer: the organization or lab that should be responsible for this result. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.quantity: the number of copies of the sequence of interest. (rnaseq). [STOP]
MolecularSequence.referenceSeq: a sequence that is used as a reference to describe variants that are present in a sequence analyzed. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.referenceSeq.chromosome: structural unit composed of a nucleic acid molecule which controls its own replication through the interaction of specific proteins at one or more origins of replication (so:0000340). [STOP]
MolecularSequence.referenceSeq.genomeBuild: the genome build used for reference, following grch build versions e.g. 'grch 37'.  version number must be included if a versioned release of a primary build was used. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.referenceSeq.orientation: a relative reference to a dna strand based on gene orientation. the strand that contains the open reading frame of the gene is the "sense" strand, and the opposite complementary strand is the "antisense" strand. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.referenceSeq.referenceSeqId: reference identifier of reference sequence submitted to ncbi. it must match the type in the molecularsequence.type field. for example, the prefix, âng_â identifies reference sequence for genes, ânm_â for messenger rna transcripts, and ânp_â for amino acid sequences. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.referenceSeq.referenceSeqPointer: a pointer to another molecularsequence entity as reference sequence. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.referenceSeq.referenceSeqString: a string like "acgt". [STOP]
MolecularSequence.referenceSeq.strand: an absolute reference to a strand. the watson strand is the strand whose 5'-end is on the short arm of the chromosome, and the crick strand as the one whose 5'-end is on the long arm. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.referenceSeq.windowStart: start position of the window on the reference sequence. if the coordinate system is either 0-based or 1-based, then start position is inclusive. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.referenceSeq.windowEnd: end position of the window on the reference sequence. if the coordinate system is 0-based then end is exclusive and does not include the last position. if the coordinate system is 1-base, then end is inclusive and includes the last position. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.variant: the definition of variant here originates from sequence ontology (variant_of). this element can represent amino acid or nucleic sequence change(including insertion,deletion,snp,etc.)  it can represent some complex mutation or segment variation with the assist of cigar string. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.variant.start: start position of the variant on the  reference sequence. if the coordinate system is either 0-based or 1-based, then start position is inclusive. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.variant.end: end position of the variant on the reference sequence. if the coordinate system is 0-based then end is exclusive and does not include the last position. if the coordinate system is 1-base, then end is inclusive and includes the last position. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.variant.observedAllele: an allele is one of a set of coexisting sequence variants of a gene (so:0001023).  nucleotide(s)/amino acids from start position of sequence to stop position of sequence on the positive (+) strand of the observed  sequence. when the sequence  type is dna, it should be the sequence on the positive (+) strand. this will lay in the range between variant.start and variant.end. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.variant.referenceAllele: an allele is one of a set of coexisting sequence variants of a gene (so:0001023). nucleotide(s)/amino acids from start position of sequence to stop position of sequence on the positive (+) strand of the reference sequence. when the sequence  type is dna, it should be the sequence on the positive (+) strand. this will lay in the range between variant.start and variant.end. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.variant.cigar: extended cigar string for aligning the sequence with reference bases. see detailed documentationhere. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.variant.variantPointer: a pointer to an observation containing variant information. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.observedSeq: sequence that was observed. it is the result marked by referenceseq along with variant records on referenceseq. this shall start from referenceseq.windowstart and end by referenceseq.windowend. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.quality: an experimental feature attribute that defines the quality of the feature in a quantitative way, such as a phred quality score (so:0001686). [STOP]
MolecularSequence.quality.type: indel / snp / undefined variant. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.quality.standardSequence: gold standard sequence used for comparing against. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.quality.start: start position of the sequence. if the coordinate system is either 0-based or 1-based, then start position is inclusive. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.quality.end: end position of the sequence. if the coordinate system is 0-based then end is exclusive and does not include the last position. if the coordinate system is 1-base, then end is inclusive and includes the last position. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.quality.score: the score of an experimentally derived feature such as a p-value (so:0001685). [STOP]
MolecularSequence.quality.method: which method is used to get sequence quality. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.quality.truthTP: true positives, from the perspective of the truth data, i.e. the number of sites in the truth call set for which there are paths through the query call set that are consistent with all of the alleles at this site, and for which there is an accurate genotype call for the event. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.quality.queryTP: true positives, from the perspective of the query data, i.e. the number of sites in the query call set for which there are paths through the truth call set that are consistent with all of the alleles at this site, and for which there is an accurate genotype call for the event. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.quality.truthFN: false negatives, i.e. the number of sites in the truth call set for which there is no path through the query call set that is consistent with all of the alleles at this site, or sites for which there is an inaccurate genotype call for the event. sites with correct variant but incorrect genotype are counted here. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.quality.queryFP: false positives, i.e. the number of sites in the query call set for which there is no path through the truth call set that is consistent with this site. sites with correct variant but incorrect genotype are counted here. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.quality.gtFP: the number of false positives where the non-ref alleles in the truth and query call sets match (i.e. cases where the truth is 1/1 and the query is 0/1 or similar). [STOP]
MolecularSequence.quality.precision: query.tp / (query.tp + query.fp). [STOP]
MolecularSequence.quality.recall: truth.tp / (truth.tp + truth.fn). [STOP]
MolecularSequence.quality.fScore: harmonic mean of recall and precision, computed as: 2 * precision * recall / (precision + recall). [STOP]
MolecularSequence.quality.roc: receiver operator characteristic (roc) curve  to give sensitivity/specificity tradeoff. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.quality.roc.score: invidual data point representing the gq (genotype quality) score threshold. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.quality.roc.numTP: the number of true positives if the gq score threshold was set to "score" field value. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.quality.roc.numFP: the number of false positives if the gq score threshold was set to "score" field value. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.quality.roc.numFN: the number of false negatives if the gq score threshold was set to "score" field value. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.quality.roc.precision: calculated precision if the gq score threshold was set to "score" field value. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.quality.roc.sensitivity: calculated sensitivity if the gq score threshold was set to "score" field value. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.quality.roc.fMeasure: calculated fscore if the gq score threshold was set to "score" field value. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.readCoverage: coverage (read depth or depth) is the average number of reads representing a given nucleotide in the reconstructed sequence. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.repository: configurations of the external repository. the repository shall store target's observedseq or records related with target's observedseq. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.repository.type: click and see / restful api / need login to see / restful api with authentication / other ways to see resource. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.repository.url: uri of an external repository which contains further details about the genetics data. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.repository.name: uri of an external repository which contains further details about the genetics data. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.repository.datasetId: id of the variant in this external repository. the server will understand how to use this id to call for more info about datasets in external repository. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.repository.variantsetId: id of the variantset in this external repository. the server will understand how to use this id to call for more info about variantsets in external repository. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.repository.readsetId: id of the read in this external repository. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.pointer: pointer to next atomic sequence which at most contains one variant. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.structureVariant: information about chromosome structure variation. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.structureVariant.variantType: information about chromosome structure variation dna change type. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.structureVariant.exact: used to indicate if the outer and inner start-end values have the same meaning. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.structureVariant.length: length of the variant chromosome. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.structureVariant.outer: structural variant outer. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.structureVariant.outer.start: structural variant outer start. if the coordinate system is either 0-based or 1-based, then start position is inclusive. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.structureVariant.outer.end: structural variant outer end. if the coordinate system is 0-based then end is exclusive and does not include the last position. if the coordinate system is 1-base, then end is inclusive and includes the last position. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.structureVariant.inner: structural variant inner. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.structureVariant.inner.start: structural variant inner start. if the coordinate system is either 0-based or 1-based, then start position is inclusive. [STOP]
MolecularSequence.structureVariant.inner.end: structural variant inner end. if the coordinate system is 0-based then end is exclusive and does not include the last position. if the coordinate system is 1-base, then end is inclusive and includes the last position. [STOP]

documentation.MolecularSequence: raw data describing a biological sequence.
documentation.MolecularSequence.identifier: a unique identifier for this particular sequence instance. this is a fhir-defined id.
documentation.MolecularSequence.type: amino acid sequence/ dna sequence / rna sequence.
documentation.MolecularSequence.coordinateSystem: whether the sequence is numbered starting at 0 (0-based numbering or coordinates, inclusive start, exclusive end) or starting at 1 (1-based numbering, inclusive start and inclusive end).
documentation.MolecularSequence.patient: the patient whose sequencing results are described by this resource.
documentation.MolecularSequence.device: the method for sequencing, for example, chip information.
documentation.MolecularSequence.performer: the organization or lab that should be responsible for this result.
documentation.MolecularSequence.quantity: the number of copies of the sequence of interest. (rnaseq).
documentation.MolecularSequence.referenceSeq: a sequence that is used as a reference to describe variants that are present in a sequence analyzed.
documentation.MolecularSequence.referenceSeq.chromosom: structural unit composed of a nucleic acid molecule which controls its own replication through the interaction of specific proteins at one or more origins of replication (so:0000340).
documentation.MolecularSequence.referenceSeq.genomeBui: the genome build used for reference, following grch build versions e.g. 'grch 37'.  version number must be included if a versioned release of a primary build was used.
documentation.MolecularSequence.referenceSeq.orientati: a relative reference to a dna strand based on gene orientation. the strand that contains the open reading frame of the gene is the "sense" strand, and the opposite complementary strand is the "antisense" strand.
documentation.MolecularSequence.referenceSeq.reference: reference identifier of reference sequence submitted to ncbi. it must match the type in the molecularsequence.type field. for example, the prefix, âng_â identifies reference sequence for genes, ânm_â for messenger rna transcripts, and ânp_â for amino acid sequences.
a pointer to another molecularsequence entity as reference sequence.
documentation.MolecularSequence.referenceSeq.strand: an absolute reference to a strand. the watson strand is the strand whose 5'-end is on the short arm of the chromosome, and the crick strand as the one whose 5'-end is on the long arm.
documentation.MolecularSequence.referenceSeq.windowSta: start position of the window on the reference sequence. if the coordinate system is either 0-based or 1-based, then start position is inclusive.
documentation.MolecularSequence.referenceSeq.windowEnd: end position of the window on the reference sequence. if the coordinate system is 0-based then end is exclusive and does not include the last position. if the coordinate system is 1-base, then end is inclusive and includes the last position.
documentation.MolecularSequence.variant: the definition of variant here originates from sequence ontology (variant_of). this element can represent amino acid or nucleic sequence change(including insertion,deletion,snp,etc.)  it can represent some complex mutation or segment variation with the assist of cigar string.
documentation.MolecularSequence.variant.start: start position of the variant on the  reference sequence. if the coordinate system is either 0-based or 1-based, then start position is inclusive.
documentation.MolecularSequence.variant.end: end position of the variant on the reference sequence. if the coordinate system is 0-based then end is exclusive and does not include the last position. if the coordinate system is 1-base, then end is inclusive and includes the last position.
documentation.MolecularSequence.variant.observedAllele: an allele is one of a set of coexisting sequence variants of a gene (so:0001023).  nucleotide(s)/amino acids from start position of sequence to stop position of sequence on the positive (+) strand of the observed  sequence. when the sequence  type is dna, it should be the sequence on the positive (+) strand. this will lay in the range between variant.start and variant.end.
documentation.MolecularSequence.variant.referenceAllel: an allele is one of a set of coexisting sequence variants of a gene (so:0001023). nucleotide(s)/amino acids from start position of sequence to stop position of sequence on the positive (+) strand of the reference sequence. when the sequence  type is dna, it should be the sequence on the positive (+) strand. this will lay in the range between variant.start and variant.end.
documentation.MolecularSequence.variant.cigar: extended cigar string for aligning the sequence with reference bases. see detailed documentationhere.
documentation.MolecularSequence.variant.variantPointer: a pointer to an observation containing variant information.
documentation.MolecularSequence.observedSeq: sequence that was observed. it is the result marked by referenceseq along with variant records on referenceseq. this shall start from referenceseq.windowstart and end by referenceseq.windowend.
documentation.MolecularSequence.quality: an experimental feature attribute that defines the quality of the feature in a quantitative way, such as a phred quality score (so:0001686).
documentation.MolecularSequence.quality.standardSequen: gold standard sequence used for comparing against.
documentation.MolecularSequence.quality.start: start position of the sequence. if the coordinate system is either 0-based or 1-based, then start position is inclusive.
documentation.MolecularSequence.quality.end: end position of the sequence. if the coordinate system is 0-based then end is exclusive and does not include the last position. if the coordinate system is 1-base, then end is inclusive and includes the last position.
documentation.MolecularSequence.quality.score: the score of an experimentally derived feature such as a p-value (so:0001685).
documentation.MolecularSequence.quality.method: which method is used to get sequence quality.
documentation.MolecularSequence.quality.truthTP: true positives, from the perspective of the truth data, i.e. the number of sites in the truth call set for which there are paths through the query call set that are consistent with all of the alleles at this site, and for which there is an accurate genotype call for the event.
documentation.MolecularSequence.quality.queryTP: true positives, from the perspective of the query data, i.e. the number of sites in the query call set for which there are paths through the truth call set that are consistent with all of the alleles at this site, and for which there is an accurate genotype call for the event.
documentation.MolecularSequence.quality.truthFN: false negatives, i.e. the number of sites in the truth call set for which there is no path through the query call set that is consistent with all of the alleles at this site, or sites for which there is an inaccurate genotype call for the event. sites with correct variant but incorrect genotype are counted here.
documentation.MolecularSequence.quality.queryFP: false positives, i.e. the number of sites in the query call set for which there is no path through the truth call set that is consistent with this site. sites with correct variant but incorrect genotype are counted here.
documentation.MolecularSequence.quality.gtFP: the number of false positives where the non-ref alleles in the truth and query call sets match (i.e. cases where the truth is 1/1 and the query is 0/1 or similar).
documentation.MolecularSequence.quality.fScore: harmonic mean of recall and precision, computed as: 2 * precision * recall / (precision + recall).
documentation.MolecularSequence.quality.roc: receiver operator characteristic (roc) curve  to give sensitivity/specificity tradeoff.
documentation.MolecularSequence.quality.roc.score: invidual data point representing the gq (genotype quality) score threshold.
documentation.MolecularSequence.quality.roc.numTP: the number of true positives if the gq score threshold was set to "score" field value.
documentation.MolecularSequence.quality.roc.numFP: the number of false positives if the gq score threshold was set to "score" field value.
documentation.MolecularSequence.quality.roc.numFN: the number of false negatives if the gq score threshold was set to "score" field value.
documentation.MolecularSequence.quality.roc.precision: calculated precision if the gq score threshold was set to "score" field value.
documentation.MolecularSequence.quality.roc.sensitivit: calculated sensitivity if the gq score threshold was set to "score" field value.
documentation.MolecularSequence.quality.roc.fMeasure: calculated fscore if the gq score threshold was set to "score" field value.
documentation.MolecularSequence.readCoverage: coverage (read depth or depth) is the average number of reads representing a given nucleotide in the reconstructed sequence.
documentation.MolecularSequence.repository: configurations of the external repository. the repository shall store target's observedseq or records related with target's observedseq.
documentation.MolecularSequence.repository.type: click and see / restful api / need login to see / restful api with authentication / other ways to see resource.
documentation.MolecularSequence.repository.url: uri of an external repository which contains further details about the genetics data.
documentation.MolecularSequence.repository.name: uri of an external repository which contains further details about the genetics data.
documentation.MolecularSequence.repository.datasetId: id of the variant in this external repository. the server will understand how to use this id to call for more info about datasets in external repository.
documentation.MolecularSequence.repository.variantsetI: id of the variantset in this external repository. the server will understand how to use this id to call for more info about variantsets in external repository.
documentation.MolecularSequence.repository.readsetId: id of the read in this external repository.
documentation.MolecularSequence.pointer: pointer to next atomic sequence which at most contains one variant.
documentation.MolecularSequence.structureVariant.varia: information about chromosome structure variation dna change type.
documentation.MolecularSequence.structureVariant.exact: used to indicate if the outer and inner start-end values have the same meaning.
documentation.MolecularSequence.structureVariant.outer: structural variant outer start. if the coordinate system is either 0-based or 1-based, then start position is inclusive.

structural variant outer end. if the coordinate system is 0-based then end is exclusive and does not include the last position. if the coordinate system is 1-base, then end is inclusive and includes the last position.
documentation.MolecularSequence.structureVariant.inner: structural variant inner start. if the coordinate system is either 0-based or 1-based, then start position is inclusive.

structural variant inner end. if the coordinate system is 0-based then end is exclusive and does not include the last position. if the coordinate system is 1-base, then end is inclusive and includes the last position.
